redshiftserverless.workgroup.workgroup.C: The collection of computing resources from which an endpoint is created.

redshiftserverless.workgroup.workgroup.B.Description: The base data warehouse capacity of the workgroup in Redshift Processing Units (RPUs).

redshiftserverless.workgroup.workgroup.C.Description: An array of parameters to set for advanced control over a database. The options are `auto_mv`, `datestyle`, `enable_case_sensitivity_identifier`, `enable_user_activity_logging`, `query_group`, , `search_path`, and query monitoring metrics that let you define performance boundaries. For more information about query monitoring rules and available metrics, see [ Query monitoring metrics for Amazon Redshift Serverless](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/redshift/latest/dg/cm-c-wlm-query-monitoring-rules.html#cm-c-wlm-query-monitoring-metrics-serverless).
The creation date of the workgroup.

redshiftserverless.workgroup.workgroup.E.Description: The endpoint that is created from the workgroup.
The value that specifies whether to enable enhanced virtual private cloud (VPC) routing, which forces Amazon Redshift Serverless to route traffic through your VPC.

redshiftserverless.workgroup.workgroup.N.Description: The namespace the workgroup is associated with.

redshiftserverless.workgroup.workgroup.P.Description: A value that specifies whether the workgroup can be accessible from a public network

redshiftserverless.workgroup.workgroup.S.Description: An array of security group IDs to associate with the workgroup.
An array of subnet IDs the workgroup is associated with.

redshiftserverless.workgroup.workgroup.W.Description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that links to the workgroup.
The unique identifier of the workgroup.
